name: Zao Jun

visual_attributes.morphology: humanoid figure often depicted in traditional Chinese attire
distinctive_features: paper effigy or plaque above the fireplace
sometimes shown with a fire poker, symbolizing 'Zhang Lang's Leg'
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: typically small, suitable for placement above a household stove
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: effigy or plaque positioned above the kitchen hearth

domain_specific_visual.associated_items: fire poker, honey smeared on lips of effigy

functional_attributes.primary_function: protector of the hearth and family
secondary_functions: reporter of household activities to the Jade Emperor
symbol of morality and family conduct

domain_specific_functional.annual_ritual: returns to Heaven to report to the Jade Emperor before Chinese New Year
domain_specific_functional.festival_association: Kitchen God festival or Little New Year

relationships.description: Zao Jun reports the household's activities to the Jade Emperor annually.
Zao Jun's wife records the household's activities for his report.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: origin stories date back to around the 2nd century BC
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: central figure in household worship, symbolizing family unity and morality

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic kitchen deity without specific cultural ties
NOT a deity associated with culinary arts
NOT a deity without a reporting function to a higher authority
